Empires & Puzzles Edd Review: The 3-Star Malosi

Edd might look like he just lost a fight with a chiropractor, but don't underestimate this 3-star thug. With Fast mana and a unique buff-blocking skill, he's basically a pocket-sized Malosi for your tournament teams. Discover why this League of Villains reject deserves a spot on your roster.
Edd: The Thick-Headed Thug Who Stops Buffs Cold
Edd: The "Malosi-Lite" Thug of the 3-Star Arena
Meet Edd, the 3-star Dark hero from the

event. Released back in early 2021, Edd was introduced as part of the ragtag group of baddies meant to challenge the classic heroes. While the 5-star villains like Dark Lord stole the spotlight, Edd quietly offered a very specific, tactical utility:

In the world of 3-star tournaments, where heroes like Gunnar and Faiez dominate with powerful buffs, having a Fast-speed counter is invaluable. Think of him as the "Malosi" of the Rare tier—he hits one target and tells them to sit down and be quiet. He is a base hero with no costume currently available, so this review focuses entirely on his original thug life form.

Let's address the elephant in the room: Edd's posture. The community has affectionately (and sometimes horrifiedly) pointed out that he looks like his neck was snapped by Steven Seagal. His expression is a mix of crazed determination and confusion, fitting for a skill named "Crazed Lunge." He captures the "low-level henchman" vibe perfectly—he's not the mastermind; he's the guy the mastermind sends to get punched first. It's goofy, it's weird, and it's honestly part of his charm.
When Edd was released, his stats were considered quite high for a 3-star hero.
Still respectable. It sits comfortably above classic snipers like Balthazar (472) but falls short of modern powerhouses like Buster or Dawn who push higher limits.
Average. He isn't made of glass, but he certainly isn't a tank.

In the current meta, his stats have aged surprisingly well. While he isn't a stat monster compared to the latest Construct or Astral Elf heroes, his attack stat is high enough to make his snipe sting, especially when fully emblemed and Limit Broken.
The skill has seen a buff since his release, moving from 290% to a solid
335% at Fast speed is standard sniper territory. It's enough to severely dent or kill a squishy enemy, especially if you bring a defense down hero.

Buff immunity on a target prevents

from receiving buffs. If you hit Gunnar, he can still cast his skill, and his allies will still get the buffs (unless you hit them too). Edd is best used to neutralize a specific threat that relies on self-buffs or to tag a key enemy before a team-wide buffer fires.

It is a surgical tool. Unlike a dispeller (Tyrum) who removes buffs

Rough Estimate: He hits harder than a non-costumed Balthazar and rivals many modern snipers. He won't one-shot a tank, but he will finish off a wing.

Upon release, Edd was seen as a niche toy. Players debated if he was worth leveling over Tyrum. The general consensus was "Yes for Rush, maybe for Buff Booster."

is timeless. Buff Immunity is a status effect that completely shuts down certain heroes.

Edd's job is to kill or neutralize. If he hits like a wet noodle, his buff block might not matter because the enemy kills him first.
Can quickly shut down key buffers before they fire.
One of the few 3-star sources of Buff Immunity.
Pierce talent helps damage push through active defensive buffs (like Kai's).
Only affects one enemy. Against team-wide buffers (Gunnar), you only stop one person from getting the buff.
Average bulk makes him vulnerable to slash attacks.
His best mode. Preventing buffs means preventing the enemy from gaining the tournament attack boost.
Healing is disabled, so damage and damage prevention are king. He hits hard enough to be relevant.
Useful against bosses who rely on annoying buffs (Counter-attack, Defense Up) if you time it right.
Treevil slows them down, Edd picks off the cleanser or buffer, Maeve drops defense, and the snipers clean up.
Kvasir stops minions, Edd stops buffs. Between the two of them, the enemy team can't do anything but slash attack.

Heroes with "Resist Ailment" capabilities can shrug off his Crazed Lunge effect.
is generally more versatile because Dispel + Cleanse is universally useful. However, Edd is a fantastic secondary project. Keep him, max him, but maybe prioritize your healers and dispellers first.

You likely have a roster full of 3-star monsters. Edd is a "luxury" pick for tournaments. If you hate fighting Faiez or Grevle tanks in Rush, Edd offers a way to neuter them early (if you get the tiles). He’s worth a roster slot, but maybe not 2LB unless you are a tournament fanatic.
"It’s like his neck has been broken by Steven Seagal."
The art has been a meme since day one.
"Edd should have had EDD (Elemental Defense Down)!"
A missed opportunity for a pun, indeed.
"I kind of like to think of him as a 3-star Malosi in a slightly tweaked way."
This remains the most accurate description of his playstyle.

Edd is the definition of a "toolbox hero." You won't use him every day, and he won't win you the match with brute force alone. But when you're staring down a team that relies heavily on a specific buff sequence, Edd is the wrench you throw into their gears. He's ugly, he's hunched, and he's incredibly effective at being a nuisance.
A solid Keeper for Tournament enthusiasts. Level him up, aim for the throat (or the neck?), and enjoy the silence of a buff-less enemy.
A comprehensive review of Edd, the 3-star Dark hero from the League of Villains. Is this fast sniper with buff blocking capability worth your mats? Find out here.
